content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Wayne K. Lemieux 
Attorney at Law 
Helm & Lemieux 
141 Duesenberg Dr., unit 5 
December 10, 1987 
Westlake Village, CA 
91362-3416 
Re: 
Our File No. I-87-286 
Dear Mr. Lemieux: 
Your letter dated November loth to Chairman Larson has been 
referred to me for response. 
You are correct in your assertion that the commission will 
now act as the code reviewing body for the Central Basin 
Municipal Water District due to its recent annexation of a 
small portion of Orange County. 
As you are obviously aware, 
the commission is the code reviewing body for all local 
government agencies with jurisdiction in more than one county. 
(Government Code section 820ll(a).) 
This provision of the 
Politic~l Reform Act eliminates any question as to the 
appropriate procedures for review of local conflict of interest 
codes for public agencies falling within more than one 
jurisdiction. 
. 
We feel that to amend the law or to provide exeptions 
through regulation would only create, rather than reduce 
confusion. 
If there are any specific questions you feel the 
local officials within the jurisdiction of the water district 
would like answered by the commission relative to the local 
code, we would be happy to respond. 
Sincerely, 
Diane Griffiths

(605) 495-4770 
(616) 446-6126 
CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURE: 
CENTRAL BASIN MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT 
As a result of a recent annexation, the Central Basin Municipal Water 
District now includes approximately 1.6 acres of territory within Orange 
County. 
The District will begin dealing with the Fair Political Practices 
Commission as a multi-county agency. 
The multi-county provisions of the 
Political Reform Act probably should not be applied under such 
circumstances. 
Local officials will be confused because the Orange County 
part of the District is obscure. 
The FPPC will be given unnecessary added 
responsibility. 
Would the FPPC be interested in adopting a regulation or 
sponsoring legislation to address this issue? 
I trust you continue to find your post interesting and challenging. 
Hope 
you are well. 
Promise to visit next time I can't avoid a trip North. 
HELM &

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
November 18, 1987 
Wayne K. Lemieux 
141 Duesenberg Drive, Unit 5 
Westlake Village, CA 
91362-3416 
Re: 
87-286 
Dear Mr. Lemieux: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on November 12, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Lilly spitz, an attorney in the 
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours, 
~ 
/11, 7J LLtt_'i'Ct-/J . 
Diane M. Griffiths

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC should adopt regulations or legislation to exempt the Central Basin Municipal Water District from multi-county agency provisions given its minimal annexation of approximately 1.6 acres in Orange County.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC affirmed that it is the proper code reviewing body for multi-county agencies under Government Code section 82011(a), and declined to amend the law or adopt exceptions through regulation, concluding that doing so would create rather than reduce confusion.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (dated Nov 10) and FPPC responses (dated Dec 10 and Nov 18). Classified as FPPC response. No formal Q/A or CONCLUSION sections present, but the December 10 letter from General Counsel contains substantive response to the request.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the FPPC should adopt regulations or legislation to exempt the Central Basin Municipal Water District from multi-county agency provisions given its minimal annexation of approximately 1.6 acres in Orange County.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC affirmed that it is the proper code reviewing body for multi-county agencies under Government Code section 82011(a), and declined to amend the law or adopt exceptions through regulation, concluding that doing so would create rather than reduce confusion.

embedding.summary: An attorney representing the Central Basin Municipal Water District requested that the FPPC consider exempting or modifying multi-county provisions of the Political Reform Act for the District following its annexation of a small Orange County parcel. The FPPC responded that it would serve as the code reviewing body for all multi-county agencies and declined to amend the law or create exceptions.
